accepted.reason: Sentence-boundary rule scanning and annotation loops are practical token-processing kernels with clear optimization structure.
Grid-wise squared-error aggregation over nested records is useful for loop optimization tasks.
Gaussian profile generation uses dense numeric loops and math-heavy kernels suitable for Cython speedups.
Streaming online-variance updates are compact numeric kernels with stateful iterative updates.
Trustworthiness/continuity/LCMC metrics provide matrix-style aggregation loops with optimization value.
Dynamic array growth patterns capture memory-management behavior that is useful for systems-style optimization examples.

skipped.reason: Binding/introspection constants and wrapper plumbing rather than a focused compute kernel.
Cython attribute-assignment test fixture, not optimization-target workload.
CEF integration callback plumbing is framework-specific glue, not standalone numeric/algorithmic kernel.
Dynamic attribute behavior tests are not representative of Cython performance optimization tasks.
